mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender D200 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned D200 engine typically costs £2,000–£5,800 supply only depending on which engine era your Defender has — the early 204DTD four-cylinder sits at £2,000–£3,500, while the later AJ20D6 six-cylinder MHEV runs £4,500–£8,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender D200 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which of the two engine eras your D200 has. The 2020 204DTD four-cylinder has documented timing chain vulnerability — treat any cold-start rattle as urgent. The 2021+ AJ20D6 six-cylinder MHEV is more refined and robust, with only minor recall-covered issues (oil filter housing, FEAD pulley). Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Defender D200?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on the 2020 204DTD four-cylinder (60k–90k miles), EGR and DPF clogging on the same engine, and oil filter housing cracking on the 2021+ AJ20D6 six-cylinder MHEV. Most AJ20D6 issues are recall-covered — check with your dealer.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender D200 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £35,000 D200, a £5,800 recon is only 17% of the car's value — absolutely worth it. These are high-value vehicles that justify replacement spend across both engine eras.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Defender D200 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: 2020 is 204DTD (2.0L four-cylinder), 2021-onward is AJ20D6 (3.0L six-cylinder MHEV). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 204DTD timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,200–£2,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap requiring a full engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Defender D200 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current AJ20D6 six-cylinder generation, the most common D200 quote request today. Older 204DTD four-cylinder pricing runs lower — see the Era Map above for era-specific figures.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender D200 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Six-cylinder AJ20D6 requires additional time for MHEV system integration.

cta.label: → Compare Defender D200 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: 204DTD Timing Chain Stretch (2020 four-cylinder only)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle, metallic ticking from engine, engine warning light, loss of power.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: at first sign of rattle, book inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The 204DTD is an interference engine — if the timing chain stretches too far and jumps a tooth, pistons strike valves and the engine is effectively destroyed. JLR's own guidance recommends chain inspection between 70,000 and 100,000 miles. If you own a 2020 Defender D200, treat any cold-start rattle as an urgent warning.

problems.title: 204DTD EGR Valve and DPF Clogging (2020 four-cylinder only)
problems.symptoms: Reduced performance, poor fuel economy, engine management light, DPF warning.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warning lights appear.

problems.recommendation: The 204DTD's EGR valve and DPF are sensitive to short urban journeys. Regular motorway runs to allow passive DPF regeneration will significantly extend component life. If you primarily drive in town, consider an EGR clean at 80,000 miles as preventative maintenance.

problems.title: AJ20D6 Oil Filter Housing Cracking (2021+ six-cylinder MHEV)
problems.symptoms: Oil leak under vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil pressure warning, oil in engine bay.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — material defect identified by JLR.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; ignored leaks can lead to oil starvation and engine failure.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak spotted.

problems.recommendation: JLR has issued recalls for AJ20D6 oil filter housing cracking — check whether your Defender has had the recall work completed. The incorrect specification of plastic used in the housing can crack over time, leading to oil leaks onto the engine and into the engine bay. This is a straightforward fix when caught early.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender D200 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Simple replacement — check recall completion first
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

rows.problem: AJ20D6 FEAD belt idler pulley failure
rows.whenItMakesSense: Recall covers many vehicles — check with your dealer first

buyingChecks.title: Buying This D200? Check These First
buyIf: * 2021+ AJ20D6 six-cylinder — more refined and robust than the four-cylinder
* Confirmed oil filter housing recall completed on any AJ20D6 example
* Full service history with 0W-20 oil meeting JLR specification
avoidIf: * 2020 204DTD four-cylinder with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* No proof of recall work completed on AJ20D6 oil filter housing or FEAD pulley
* Poor service history on any generation — these Ingenium engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis, regardless of engine era

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The D200 badge is really two different cars — the 2020 four-cylinder 204DTD demands timing chain vigilance and short-journey awareness, while the 2021+ six-cylinder AJ20D6 is a more refined, more robust proposition with only minor (and recall-covered) issues. Buy on documented history and recall completion, not on badge name alone.

cta.label: → Compare Defender D200 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender D200 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle on startup (timing chain warning on 204DTD four-cylinder)
* Service History — Including which engine era — see the map above (204DTD vs AJ20D6)
* Oil Leak Check — Look under the vehicle and around the engine bay for oil (AJ20D6 housing recall)
* Recall Completion — Verify oil filter housing and FEAD pulley recalls done on AJ20D6
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on both eras
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
